summary: Stellar second set with soaring Stella Blue and tight jams
blurb: The show delivers a powerful second‑set flow highlighted by The Other One → Stella Blue, an inspired Franklin’s Tower, and a killer Let It Grow > Deal stretch. Jerry’s phrasing on Stella Blue shines while Brent’s keyboards add sparkle throughout. Recommended for anyone looking for a solid early‑’80s Dead performance.
review: On March 6 1981 at Pittsburgh’s intimate Stanley Theatre the Dead were in top form. The first set opened with a crisp Jack Straw and rolled through classics like Peggy‑O and Row Jimmy, but it was the second set that truly lifted the night – Samson & Delilah roared into It Must Have Been The Roses, then an unforgettable Estimated Prophet → Franklin’s Tower segue unfolded before a hot drum duel and a seamless The Other One → Stella Blue transition. The band kept the energy high through Sugar Magnolia, and the encore “Don’t Ease Me In” closed the night on a warm note. Jerry Garcia delivered emotional depth on Stella Blue and fiery leads on Franklin’s Tower, while Phil Lesh’s legendary 13‑note bass intro and his “bomblets” in Roses added that signature improvisational sparkle. Brent Mydland was on fire, laying down a standout Moog solo during Little Red Rooster and keeping the funk alive on Let It Grow > Deal. Both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann pounded out a solid, driving groove that propelled the jams forward. For listeners seeking the most complete and lively capture of this night, the audience tape gd81-03-06.wise.lutch.3921.sbeok.shnf offers excellent overall balance with enough crowd ambience to feel right at home in the Stanley.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All eight analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the same highlights (Stella Blue, The Other One, Franklin’s Tower, Let It Grow > Deal) and noting strong band interplay. No negative comments about performance appear, just minor recording‑type observations, so a strong positive adjustment is warranted.

best_recording.reason: Provides a full‑show capture with good‑to‑excellent audience sound, clear instrument separation and includes all the standout moments highlighted across recordings.

key_highlights: The Other One → Stella Blue transition delivers an emotional peak.
Let It Grow > Deal showcases Brent’s funky keyboard work and tight band chemistry.
Estimated Prophet → Franklin’s Tower rare segue adds excitement to the second set.
Strong drum performance early in the second set drives the jams forward.
song_highlights: It Must Have Been The Roses

band_performance.Jerry: Emotional phrasing on Stella Blue, wild solos on Franklin’s Tower and a killer Jack Straw opener; great tone and superb playing throughout the set.
band_performance.Phil: Legendary 13‑note bass riff opening Samson & Delilah and "bomblets" in Roses showcase his classic improvisational flair.
band_performance.Brent: Notable Moog/keyboard solo on Little Red Rooster, accurate clavinet patches and overall brilliance; shines especially during Let It Grow > Deal.
band_performance.Mickey: Provides hot, driving percussion early in the second set, keeping the groove tight.
band_performance.Billy: Pairs with Mickey to deliver powerful drumming throughout, particularly on the climactic jams.

must_listen_sequences: It Must Have Been The Roses
